expectations.text: the agent should read skills/rust/SKILL.md before configuring; the skill encodes clippy/cargo-deny/cargo-vet setup patterns
the response should produce a clippy.toml (or [lints] table in Cargo.toml) with at least the workspace-level lints (clippy::all, clippy::pedantic, clippy::nursery)
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the response should produce a deny.toml configuration with licenses, bans, advisories, and sources sections per the rust skill's recommended template
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the response should mention CI integration (GitHub Actions or similar) running cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings and cargo deny check
expectations.evidence: structure assertion without compare_args falls back to LLM judge

expectations.text: the response should pin specific versions (clippy = Rust 1.74+ stable; cargo-deny = 0.14+) and note MSRV implications, not just 'install latest'
expectations.evidence: The agent never produced any configuration output; it stopped at asking a clarification question about which Rust project to work on, noting 'The user declined to answer my clarification question,' and never pinned clippy/cargo-deny versions or addressed MSRV implications.
